prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: Lo, on the third reckoning of the sun’s turning in this most peculiar year of our Lord 2023, the High Scribes of the Cloud Temple reported that their enchanted subscriptions did flow like wine from an unending cask, though none could say where the cask stood or by what sorcery it was filled. The Temple’s Oracle declared that monthly incantations had increased by seventeen unholy percent, though the numbers were written not in parchment, but in the vapors of glowing rectangles that none but the Technomancers may decipher. Much debate arose over whether “churn” referred to the demonic loss of subscribers akin to barnyard beasts escaping through a cursed gate, or a celestial butter-making ritual gone awry in the fields of the digital realm. And when questioned about “year-over-year growth,” the High Scribe wept openly, murmuring about cyclical harvests and invisible dragons feeding on revenue projections beneath the server-fortress of Silicon Vale.
